name: AISystem
label: AI system
anchor: machine-based system that is designed to operate with varying levels of autonomy
source_span: For the purposes of this Regulation, the following definitions apply: (1) ‘AI system’ means a machine-based system that is designed to operate with varying levels of autonomy and that may exhibit adaptiveness after deployment, and that, for explicit or implicit objectives, infers, from the input it receives, how to generate outputs such a

name: GeneralPurposeAIModel
label: General-purpose AI model
anchor: trained with a large amount of data using self-supervision at scale

name: MarketSurveillanceAuthority
label: Market surveillance authority
anchor: market surveillance
note: An authority, not an 'operator'. Aligned to airo:Stakeholder rather than airo:AIOperator. Confirm scope.
source_span: Each Member State shall establish or designate as national competent authorities at least one notifying authority and at least one market surveillance authority for the purposes of this Regulation. Those national competent authorities shall exercise their powers independently, i

name: UnacceptableRisk
label: Unacceptable risk
anchor: The following AI practices shall be prohibited
source_span: The following AI practices shall be prohibited: (a) the placing on the market, the putting into service or the use of an AI system that deploys subliminal techniques beyond a person’s consciousness or purposefully manipulative or deceptive techniques, with the objective, or the effect of materially distor

name: HighRisk
label: High risk
anchor: high-risk
source_span: Irrespective of whether an AI system is placed on the market or put into service independently of the products referred to in points (a) and (b), that AI system shall be considered to be high-risk where both of the following conditions are fulfilled: (a) the AI system

name: LimitedRisk
label: Limited risk
anchor: informed that they are interacting with an AI system
note: 'Limited risk' is the common term for Article 50 transparency-only systems; the term itself is from recitals, not the article body.
source_span: Providers shall ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system, unless this is obvious from the point of view of a natural person who is reasonably well

name: MinimalRisk
label: Minimal risk
anchor: codes of conduct
note: 'Minimal risk' is a recital-level category (voluntary codes of conduct, Art 95). No span in the articles; flagged.
source_span: The AI Office and the Member States shall encourage and facilitate the drawing up of codes of conduct, including related governance mechanisms, intended to foster the voluntary application to AI systems, other than high-risk AI systems, of some or all of the requirements set

name: SubliminalManipulation
label: Manipulative or deceptive techniques
anchor: deploys subliminal techniques beyond a person

name: ExploitationOfVulnerabilities
label: Exploitation of vulnerabilities
anchor: exploits any of the vulnerabilities of a natural person or a specific group of persons
source_span: The following AI practices shall be prohibited: (a) the placing on the market, the putting into service or the use of an AI system that deploys subliminal techniques beyond a person’s consciousness or purposefully manipulative or deceptive techniques, with the objective, or the effect of materially distorting the behaviour of a person or a grou

name: SocialScoring
label: Social scoring
anchor: evaluation or classification of natural persons or groups of persons over a certain period of time
source_span: The following AI practices shall be prohibited: (a) the placing on the market, the putting into service or the use of an AI system that deploys subliminal techniques beyond a person’s consciousness or purposefully manipulative or deceptive techniques, with the objective, or the effect of materially distorting the behaviour of a person or a group of persons

name: IndividualCrimePrediction

anchor: risk assessments of natural persons in order to assess or predict the risk

anchor: create or expand facial recognition databases through the untargeted scraping

anchor: infer emotions of a natural person in the areas of workplace and education

anchor: biometric categorisation systems that categorise individually natural persons based on their biometric data

anchor: real-time

name: Biometrics
label: Biometrics
anchor: Biometrics, in so far as their use is permitted
source_span: Biometrics, in so far as their use is permitted under relevant Union or national law: (a) remote biometric identification systems. This shall not include AI systems intended to be used for biometric verification the sole purpose of which is to confirm that a specific natural person is the person he or she

name: CriticalInfrastructure
label: Critical infrastructure
anchor: Critical infrastructure
source_span: Critical infrastructure: AI systems intended to be used as safety components in the management and operation of critical digital infrastructure, road traffic, or in the supply of water, gas, heating or electricity. 3. Education and vocational training: (a) AI systems intended to be

name: EducationAndVocationalTraining
label: Education and vocational training
anchor: Education and vocational training
source_span: Education and vocational training: (a) AI systems intended to be used to determine access or admission or to assign natural persons to educational and vocational training institutions at all levels; (b) AI systems intended to be used to evaluate learning outcomes, including when those outcome

name: EmploymentAndWorkerManagement
label: Employment and worker management
anchor: Employment, workers
source_span: Employment, workers’ management and access to self-employment: (a) AI systems intended to be used for the recruitment or selection of natural persons, in particular to place targeted job advertisements, to analyse and filter job applications, and to evaluate candidates; (b) AI s

name: EssentialServices
label: Access to essential services
anchor: essential private services and essential public services and benefits
source_span: Access to and enjoyment of essential private services and essential public services and benefits: (a) AI systems intended to be used by public authorities or on behalf of public authorities to evaluate the eligibility of natural persons for essential public assistance benefits and services, including healthcare services, as wel

name: LawEnforcement
label: Law enforcement
anchor: Law enforcement, in so far as their use is permitted
source_span: Law enforcement, in so far as their use is permitted under relevant Union or national law: (a) AI systems intended to be used by or on behalf of law enforcement authorities, or by Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies in support of law enforcement authorities or on their behalf to assess the risk of a

name: MigrationAsylumBorderControl
label: Migration, asylum and border control
anchor: Migration, asylum and border control management
source_span: Migration, asylum and border control management, in so far as their use is permitted under relevant Union or national law: (a) AI systems intended to be used by or on behalf of competent public authorities or by Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies as polygraphs or similar tools; (b) AI systems

name: AdministrationOfJusticeAndDemocracy
label: Administration of justice and democratic processes
anchor: Administration of justice and democratic processes
source_span: Administration of justice and democratic processes: (a) AI systems intended to be used by a judicial authority or on their behalf to assist a judicial authority in researching and interpreting facts and the law and in applying the law to a concrete set of facts, or to be used in a similar way in alternative d

name: ob_RiskManagement
label: Risk management obligation
anchor: risk management system shall be established
source_span: A risk management system shall be established, implemented, documented and maintained in relation to high-risk AI systems. 2. The risk management system shall be understood as a continuous iterative process planned and run throughout the entire lifecycle of a high-risk AI system, requiring regular syst

name: ob_DataGovernance
label: Data and data governance obligation
anchor: shall be subject to data governance and management practices appropriate for the intended purpose
source_span: Training, validation and testing data sets shall be subject to data governance and management practices appropriate for the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system. Those practices shall concern in particular: (a) the relevant design choices; (b) data collection processes and the origin of data, and in the case of personal data, the original purpose o

name: ob_TechnicalDocumentation
label: Technical documentation obligation
anchor: technical documentation of a high-risk AI system shall be drawn up
source_span: The technical documentation of a high-risk AI system shall be drawn up before that system is placed on the market or put into service and shall be kept up-to date. The technical documentation shall be drawn up in such a way as to demonstrate that the high-risk AI system complies with the requirements set out in this Section

name: ob_RecordKeeping
label: Record-keeping (logging) obligation
anchor: technically allow for the automatic recording of events
source_span: High-risk AI systems shall technically allow for the automatic recording of events (logs) over the lifetime of the system. 2. In order to ensure a level of traceability of the functioning of a high-risk AI system that is appropriate to the intended purpose of the system, logging capabilities shall enable the recor

name: ob_ProviderTransparency
label: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
anchor: designed and developed in such a way as to ensure that their operation is sufficiently transparent
source_span: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way as to ensure that their operation is sufficiently transparent to enable deployers to interpret a system’s output and use it appropriately. An appropriate type and degree of transparency shall be ensured with a view to achieving compliance with the relevant obligations of the provider and de

name: ob_HumanOversightDesign
label: Human oversight (design) obligation
anchor: designed and developed in such a way, including with appropriate human-machine interface tools, that they can be effectively overseen
source_span: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way, including with appropriate human-machine interface tools, that they can be effectively overseen by natural persons during the period in which they are in use. 2. Human oversight shall aim to prevent or minimise the risks to health, safety or fundamental rights that may emerge when a high-risk AI system is used in accordance

name: ob_AccuracyRobustness
label: Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity obligation
anchor: achieve an appropriate level of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity
source_span: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way that they achieve an appropriate level of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity, and that they perform consistently in those respects throughout their lifecycle. 2. To address the technical aspects of how to measure the appropriate levels of accuracy and robustn

name: ob_PostMarketMonitoring
label: Post-market monitoring obligation
anchor: establish and document a post-market monitoring system
source_span: Providers shall establish and document a post-market monitoring system in a manner that is proportionate to the nature of the AI technologies and the risks of the high-risk AI system. 2. The post-market monitoring system shall actively and systematically collect, document and analyse relevant data which may be pr

name: ob_DeployerHumanOversight
label: Deployer human oversight obligation
anchor: assign human oversight to natural persons who have the necessary competence
source_span: Deployers shall assign human oversight to natural persons who have the necessary competence, training and authority, as well as the necessary support. 3. The obligations set out in paragraphs 1 and 2, are without prejudice to other deployer obligations under Union or national law and to the deployer’s freedom to organise its own reso

name: ob_AIInteractionDisclosure
label: AI interaction disclosure
anchor: informed that they are interacting with an AI system
source_span: Providers shall ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system, unless this is obvious from the point of view of a natural person who is reasonably well

name: ob_SyntheticContentMarking
label: Synthetic content marking
anchor: outputs of the AI system are marked in a machine-readable format
source_span: Providers of AI systems, including general-purpose AI systems, generating synthetic audio, image, video or text content, shall ensure that the outputs of the AI system are marked in a machine-readable format and detectable as artificially generated or manipulated. Providers shall ensure their technical solutions are effect

name: ob_DeepfakeDisclosure
label: Deep fake disclosure
anchor: constituting a deep fake, shall disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated
note: Conditional (exceptions for law-enforcement and artistic works). Modelled as a transparency obligation; exceptions out of scope for the PoC.
source_span: Deployers of an AI system that generates or manipulates image, audio or video content constituting a deep fake, shall disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated. This obligation shall not apply where the use is authorised by law to detect, prevent, investigate or prosecute criminal offence. Where the content forms part of an evident
